specification.topic-statement: The restore pin replay reconstructs an agent's pinned document list from database events during session restoration, starting from the fork snapshot and then replaying all subsequent pin and unpin commands in chronological order.

scope.in-scope: Fork event pinned-path snapshot loading, chronological pin/unpin command replay, duplicate detection on pin, compaction on unpin, and the independence of pin replay from conversation clear boundaries.
scope.boundary: The /pin and /unpin commands that modify the list during a live session, and how the pinned list is used to assemble the system prompt, are covered by the pinned documents specification.
The broader agent restoration orchestration is covered by the agent restore specification.

behavior.fork-snapshot-load:
Before replaying individual pin/unpin commands, the agent's fork event is queried to obtain the initial pinned document snapshot. This snapshot represents the parent's pinned list at the moment of forking. Each path in the fork event's pinned_paths JSON array is appended to the agent's pinned list.
For the root agent, the fork event contains the default initial pinned paths established at database initialization. For forked agents, it reflects the parent's state at fork time.
behavior.command-replay:
All pin and unpin command events for the agent are queried and sorted in chronological order. Each event is processed in turn:
A pin command event adds the stored path to the pinned list. Before adding, the existing list is searched for an exact match. If the path is already present (due to fork snapshot or an earlier pin command), it is not added again. If absent, it is appended and the array reallocated to exact size.
An unpin command event removes the stored path from the pinned list. A linear search by exact string match locates the entry. If found, the entry is freed, subsequent entries are shifted to fill the gap, and the array is reallocated to the new exact size. If not found, the event is silently ignored.
behavior.boundary-independence:
Pin and unpin commands are replayed across the agent's entire event history, regardless of any /clear events that may have occurred. This makes the pinned document list persistent across conversation clears and session restarts.
Unlike conversation messages (which are bounded by the most recent /clear), pinned document state accumulates all pin and unpin commands ever issued for the agent. A document pinned before a /clear remains pinned after the /clear.
